act.title: Acceptance & commitment therapy
act.description: Building psychological flexibility through six interconnected processes. We're designing this module carefully - it'll arrive in the next major release.
act.statusTitle: In design · Coming after the CBT program ships
act.statusBody: ACT teaches that psychological pain is unavoidable, but suffering can be reduced by changing your relationship with thoughts and feelings rather than their content.
act.processesDescription: The 'hexaflex' - six skills that reinforce each other. Each will get its own structured program inside the module.

defusion.name: Cognitive defusion
defusion.desc: Stepping back from thoughts instead of being fused with them. Treating thoughts as events, not commands.

acceptance.name: Acceptance
acceptance.desc: Opening to difficult feelings rather than fighting or avoiding them - willingness as a skill.

present.name: Present-moment
present.desc: Conscious contact with what is happening here and now.

selfAsContext.name: Self-as-context
selfAsContext.desc: The noticing self - bigger than any single thought or feeling.

values.name: Values
values.desc: Chosen directions that give life meaning. Not goals to achieve, ways to live.

committedAction.name: Committed action
committedAction.desc: Behavior anchored to values, even when difficult. The how of living the values.

act.toolsTitle: Shared tools you can already use
act.toolsDescription: ACT will lean on these - they're available now from the sidebar.

dbt.title: Dialectical behavior therapy
dbt.description: Skills for high emotion intensity - distress tolerance, emotion regulation, mindfulness, and interpersonal effectiveness. On the roadmap.
dbt.statusBody: DBT was developed for high-emotion-intensity experiences. It pairs acceptance with change in equal measure.

mindfulness.name: Mindfulness
mindfulness.desc: Observing, describing, participating with full attention.

distressTolerance.name: Distress tolerance
distressTolerance.desc: Surviving crisis without making it worse. TIPP, ACCEPTS, IMPROVE.

emotionRegulation.name: Emotion regulation
emotionRegulation.desc: Reducing vulnerability, understanding emotions, changing painful ones.

interpersonal.name: Interpersonal effectiveness
interpersonal.desc: Asking, saying no, and keeping the relationship and self-respect intact.
